title: Rataque VR Starter Pack
description: A modpack that improves your experience in VR. Nothing new, just the basic QoL and Performance. I made it for a few friends to use :)
body:
**Hi, I made this 1.21.5 Vivecraft modpack, to make it easier for my friends and viewers who want to play ViveCraft with a little more stability.**


**Mod list:**


<details>
<summary>Spoiler</summary>

Vivecraft

ImmersiveMC (Allows you to interact directly with crafting tables, furnaces, and chests)

MC VR API (Improves ImmersiveMC experience in VR)

Architectury API (MC VR API needs it)

Iris Shaders

Visuality

ScalableLux

Sodium Extra

Sodium

Lithium

FerriteCore

Entity Culling

MoreCulling

Noisium

Perfect Accuracy (Fix a problem with VR bow)

Reacharound (Makes building easier in VR)

Spawn Animations 

More Mobs

Falling Leaves

Krypton

ImmediatelyFast

Cloth Config API

CreativeCore

[EMF] Entity Model Features [Fabric & Forge]

[ETF] Entity Texture Features - [Fabric & Forge]

LambdaDynamicLights


</details>




**Resource Pack list:**

(use them in this order, in your game)

<details>
<summary>Spoiler</summary>

More Mob Variants x Fresh Animations

Fresh Animations

Theone's Eating Animation Pack


</details>




With these mods and Resource Packs, you'll have a little more FPS, less lag spikes and a few visual tweaks that looks beautiful in VR (Lambda Dynamic Lights, Visuality, Falling Leaves, Spawn Animations, More Mobs, and of course, Fresh Animations)

If you are playing in a high-end PC and have a lot of RAM, consider putting at least 6GB of RAM in your client. In the JVM arguments. (Installations -> Three dots -> Edit Installation -> JVM Arguments)

Steps can be found in Vivecraft Github: https://github.com/Vivecraft/VivecraftMod/wiki/Memory-and-GC-Setup

**I don't recommend using Distant Horizons in ViveCraft.**

You can try a few shaders to see if your PC can handle it.

**HOW TO PLAY IN VR**

It's really simple. You need SteamVR installed and Steam opened in your PC.
(Both are free)

Connect your VR Headset to your PC -> Open the game -> In the menu, change "VR: No" to "VR: Yes"
Put the headset on your face and start playing.

If it doesn't work, I recommend you close the game, open the game again, open SteamVR on your Steam, and then activate the VR option in the menu.

If you have any problems with Vivecraft, please consider asking for help in their [Discord](https://discord.com/invite/c4WunxQ)
